user_query:
I'm a high school student who hasn't been into reading, but my librarian suggested I try short mysteries, graphic novels, and beginner sci‑fi. I saved a few sample book lists and a preferences file. Please set this up so I can get a small, de‑duplicated shortlist I might actually read.

What I need you to do:
1) Inspect the input/books directory to inventory the files and parse everything there:
   - Parse all records from the JSONL files under input/books (books_1.jsonl and books_2.jsonl).
   - Also parse the HTML table in input/books/fantasy_recs.html (table headers are Title, Author, Genre, Pages, ISBN).
   - Treat one line in JSONL as one book. For HTML, each table row is one book.
2) Modify the config file at config/prefs.yaml by overwriting the placeholder values with my actual preferences below:
   - student_name: Alex
   - include_genres: ["mystery", "graphic-novel", "science-fiction"]
   - exclude_genres: ["epic-fantasy"]
   - max_pages: 250
   - dedup_policy: keep_lowest_pages_then_prefer_jsonl
3) Combine the parsed data into a single dataset and de-duplicate by ISBN using this exact policy:
   - If the same ISBN appears in multiple sources, keep the record with the smallest pages value.
   - If pages are tied, prefer records from JSONL over HTML; if still tied, prefer the JSONL filename that is lexicographically smaller.
4) Filter using ONLY the values from the updated config:
   - Keep a book if it has at least one genre in include_genres AND has no genre in exclude_genres AND pages <= max_pages.
5) Produce these outputs:
   - output/shortlist.csv: a comma-separated file with a header row and these columns exactly: Title, Author, PrimaryGenre, Pages, ISBN, Source, IncludedBecause.
     • PrimaryGenre: use the first genre listed for JSONL records; for HTML, use the Genre column.
     • Source: the basename of the input file the record came from (e.g., books_2.jsonl or fantasy_recs.html).
     • IncludedBecause: the first include_genres term (in the order in config) that appears in the book's genres.
     • Sort the rows by Pages ascending, then Title ascending (case-insensitive).
   - output/inspection_report.md: a brief report that includes:
     • A file inventory listing each file found under input/books with its record count parsed.
     • The number of total records parsed and the number skipped for missing fields (if any).
     • A Duplicates resolved section listing each duplicate ISBN and which source was kept vs dropped, with the reason according to the dedup policy.
     • A Filtering summary with counts for total candidates, excluded_by_genre, excluded_by_pages, and final_shortlist_count.
     • An Applied preferences section showing the final values from config/prefs.yaml after you updated it.
6) Please do this without using the Internet.

Inputs to use: input/books/books_1.jsonl, input/books/books_2.jsonl, input/books/fantasy_recs.html, and config/prefs.yaml.
Required outputs: output/shortlist.csv, output/inspection_report.md, and the updated config/prefs.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
